title: Webinar: Streamlining Nonclinical Development with FDA NAMs
snippet: FDA’s draft guidance on the validation of non-animal methods (NAMs) is reshaping how nonclinical programs are designed and justified. This webinar explores how sponsors can apply NAMs to streamline development, improve human relevance, and support IND decision-making within a weight-of-evidence framework.

title: Ipsen sends clear Memo to rare diseases with up to $796M buyout
snippet: Ipsen is penning its second acquisition of the week, this time securing Memo Therapeutics and its midstage monoclonal antibody in a deal that could approach $800 million.

title: Orca opens up Treg cell therapy with FDA nod for allogeneic blood cancer treatment
snippet: The approval of Tregzi—the first regulatory greenlight for Orca Bio—was based on a Phase 3 study in which patients on the therapy were twice as likely to survive without cancer relapse and without chronic GVHD compared with conventional allogeneic transplant.

title: Eli Lilly and Regeneron among 7 companies picked for FDA PreCheck pilot program
snippet: Teams at facilities being developed by Eli Lilly, Regeneron and other companies will receive early technical guidance and additional perks from the FDA.

title: Integrated Proteomic and scRNA-Seq Analysis Reveals Pyroptosis-Related Subtypes in Lung Adenocarcinoma.
snippet: Pyroptosis is a recently identified form of programmed cell death that plays an important role in cancer initiation and progression. However, the function of pyroptosis in lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) remains unclear. title: Elevated Body Mass Index Associated With Uterine Fibroids: A Retrospective Cohort Study With Bioinformatic Validation.
publicationName: The journal of obstetrics and gynaecology research
journalName: The journal of obstetrics and gynaecology research
snippet: Uterine fibroids are the most common benign pelvic tumors in women. Meanwhile, the prevalence of obesity has been increasing annually and affects various health conditions. title: Biochemistry at scale: Seeing both the forest and the trees.
snippet: Today, therapeutic drug discovery lies at the crossroads of mechanistic biochemistry and the rapidly growing fields of multi-omics and AI. How will traditionally reductionist biochemistry adapt to the increasing complexity of biological processes? We address this question by first examining historical cases in which biochemistry uncovered new biology in pat...

title: Cancer Heterogeneity and Cancer Cell Plasticity: Molecular Mechanisms and Precision Therapy.
snippet: Tumor heterogeneity creates selective pressures and ecological niches that enable cancer cell plasticity; conversely, plasticity continuously generates and reshapes heterogeneity. Together, these processes drive tumor progression, therapeutic resistance, recurrence, and divergent clinical outcomes, thereby limiting the durability of precision oncology. In t...

title: Methotrexate-induced hepatotoxicity-mechanism, clinical cases and treatment approaches.
snippet: Methotrexate (MTX) is a class of antifolate chemotherapeutics and immunosuppressants that are effectively used in the treatment regimen of cancer and autoimmune diseases. In the current MASLD era, methotrexate-induced hepatotoxicity must be understood in the context of host metabolic dysfunction, obesity, diabetes, and alcohol use that amplify oxidative and...

title: Overexpression of Circular RNA circTTN Promotes Ferroptosis in Sepsis-Induced Cardiomyopathy.
publicationName: Journal of cellular and molecular medicine
journalName: Journal of cellular and molecular medicine
snippet: Sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy (SCM) is one of the common complications caused by sepsis. Despite the higher mortality rate associated with SCM, the pathogenesis of SCM is poorly understood. title: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) Versus Genomic Profiling in Cancer: Roles in Precision Medicine.
snippet: Precision oncology increasingly depends on accurate biomarker assessment to guide targeted therapies. Immunohistochemistry (IHC) has long been central to routine pathology due to its accessibility and diagnostic utility; however, the expanding role of molecularly driven treatments has highlighted limitations in protein-based testing.
